specific_location: Pueblo Reservoir, seen from Sailboard launching area.
description: I was scanning the lake from Sailboard launching area with my binoculars and noticed a loon shaped bird way across the lake, out from the South Shore Marina. I then got out of the car, and got the scope out to see what it is. It was a small loon, with thin up turned bill, grayish back, and a dark throat patch. I quickly realized the bird is a Red-throated Loon. Too small for Common Loon, bill too thin and upturned for Pacific Loon. I couldn't make out that throat patch was reddish, though, from the distance the bird was from me, that wasn't surprising.Behaviors: Swimming and diving on Pueblo Reservoir.Call: nonePlumage: breeding plumage adult
